title: Urine Leakage with Coughing - Stress Urinary Incontinence
chief_complaint: 52-year-old G4P4 postmenopausal female with involuntary urine leakage when coughing, sneezing, laughing, or exercising for 2 years. No urgency or frequency. Pad use 3-4 per day. Three vaginal deliveries.

physical_exam.pelvic: Cystocele grade 2, urethral hypermobility, positive cough stress test, no rectocele

hidden_data.urodynamics: Urodynamic stress incontinence confirmed, no detrusor overactivity

patient_responses.leakage: I leak when I cough or laugh, not when I feel urge
patient_responses.deliveries: I had 3 vaginal deliveries, largest baby 4kg

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! Stress incontinence from urethral hypermobility.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is stress incontinence. The cough test + urodynamics confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: SUI: Pelvic floor PT (Kegel exercises) for 3 months first. If inadequate, mid-urethral sling (TVT or TOT). Success rate 85-90%. Vaginal estrogen if postmenopausal. Avoid caffeine and weight loss if obese.
